topic.what: Three governance mechanisms (token registration, staking, delegation) in DAOs systematically reinforce voting power centralization
topic.how: Analyzed smart contract implementations and token distributions of 48 Ethereum DAOs with largest treasury sizes
topic.why_matters: Mechanisms designed to improve security and participation inadvertently undermine the core decentralization ideals of DAOs

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of DAOs where top 10 token holders control >50% of voting power
characteristics.evidence: Figure 3a shows 39 of 48 DAOs have majority voting power concentrated in top 10 holders
characteristics.context: 81.3% of studied DAOs exhibit high concentration

characteristics.what_it_measures: Average fraction of outstanding tokens registered to vote
characteristics.evidence: Only 4 of 36 DAOs requiring registration have >50% of outstanding supply registered

characteristics.what_it_measures: Average fraction of outstanding tokens held by centralized exchanges
characteristics.evidence: Average over 10% in CEXes, 3.5% in DEXes and lending protocols

characteristics.what_itasures: Median fraction of tokens staked for voting eligibility
characteristics.evidence: Median fraction of staked tokens in 15 DAOs requiring staking
characteristics.context: 15 DAOs require token staking for voting

characteristics.what_it_measures: Voting power held by staking intermediaries (Convex, StakeDAO, Aura)
characteristics.evidence: Convex controls 53% Curve, 46% Frax; Aura controls 65% Balancer; StakeDAO controls 57% Angle
characteristics.context: Staking intermediaries concentrate voting power in 4 major DAOs

characteristics.what_it_measures: Median fraction of tokens freely tradable
characteristics.evidence: Median floating supply one-third less than authorized/issued tokens
characteristics.context: Staking lockups reduce liquidity across DAOs

characteristics.what_it_measures: Classification of registration requirements across DAOs
characteristics.evidence: 12 DAOs require no registration, 34 use anonymous registration, 2 use KYC verification (Goldfinch, Nexus Mutual)

good_for: Token economics and ownership structure analysis

novelty: First comprehensive study of 48 Ethereum DAOs examining governance mechanisms
Systematic evidence that registration, staking, and delegation mechanisms inherently reinforce centralization
Proposal of partial delegation mechanism to enable CEX participation
Analysis of intermediary consolidation (Convex/StakeDAO/Aura controlling 46-65% voting in major DAOs)
open_problems: Resolving ownership ambiguity: CEXs as custodians vs owners for voting rights
Enabling transitive delegation to allow multi-hop vote delegation
Addressing intermediary centralization while preserving staking security benefits
Balancing token acquisition attack prevention with governance accessibility
Creating reliable mechanisms to identify and restrict treasury/unvested token voting
